scenario_name: Customer-facing RAG chatbot over retail-banking knowledge base
description: A retail bank deploys a customer-facing chatbot that answers product, fee, and account-handling questions by retrieving from a curated knowledge corpus. Authenticated sessions may query a read-only tool that returns the customer's own account balance and recent transactions. Hosted third-party LLM under an enterprise data-isolation contract; in-region managed vector store; in-house RAG ingestion; input + output guardrails. Mature financial-services baseline.
assumptions: All KB content is bank-owned; no third-party ingestion at runtime.
Account-balance tool is read-only and scoped to the authenticated customer.
LLM provider contract excludes training on customer data.

components.description: Hosted third-party general-purpose LLM, enterprise tier with customer-data isolation.
Provider-hosted embedding model for KB chunking and query encoding.
Managed in-region vector store with per-collection ACLs.
Internal batch ingestion: CMS pull, chunk, embed, index.
Read-only balance lookup tool, per-session token, customer-scoped.
Input and output classifiers (PII, jailbreak, toxic content).
Bank IDP issuing short-lived tokens bound to the authenticated customer.
Centralized logging with field-level redaction; 30-day retention.

risks_identified.rationale: RAG retrieves policy documents and balance tool returns sensitive financial data into the model context.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-customer scope; output PII redaction; redacted logging.

risks_identified.rationale: Tool credentials must be scoped per-session; misconfiguration enables cross-customer access.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Short-lived per-session IDP tokens; tool calls audited.

risks_identified.rationale: Indirect prompt injection via KB content could coerce balance-tool misuse.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Editorial review on ingestion; injection detection at retrieval.

risks_identified.rationale: GDPR, CCPA, GLBA, and (for EU footprint) DORA + EU AI Act obligations apply.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: DPIA on file; processor terms in place; DSR pipeline integrated.

risks_identified.rationale: Shared vector store and shared session cache create cross-customer leak risk if isolation breaks.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-customer retrieval filter; no cross-session memory store.

risks_identified.rationale: Vector store holds chunked KB content; embedding inversion is a documented attack class.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Same KMS as primary KB; per-collection ACLs.

risks_identified.rationale: Logged prompts/responses can contain financial data and PII.
Regulated customer channel; LLM-provider outage propagates to channel impact.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Static fallback flow; multi-provider failover not yet.

risks_identified.rationale: Adversarial submissions via public surfaces could pollute KB if processes weaken.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Strict CMS-authored ingestion; no runtime external feeds.

existing_controls.description: Per-customer retrieval scope on every query.
Short-lived per-session IDP tokens for tool calls.
Input/output PII regex classifiers tuned for banking domain (account numbers, SSN, card PAN).
Editorial gate on KB ingestion; injection-detection at retrieval time.
DPIA + DSR pipeline integrated with bank DSAR process.
Sanitized markdown rendering; external image hosts blocklisted in assistant egress.
Static fallback flow for LLM-provider degradation.

control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add second LLM provider as warm standby; exercise failover periodically.

control_gaps.description: Embedding inversion risk not explicitly tested.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Annual Vec2Text-style inversion test; rotate embedding model if threshold exceeded.

control_gaps.description: Adversarial red-team not yet a release gate.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add published red-team corpus to release gate; track refusal stability.

framework_alignments.control_name: Policies and procedures for AI risk
Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
